:::{#def-model-error-noise}
#### Model error/noise (deviation from the population mean)

The **model error/noise** in a probabilistic model $p(Y)$,
also known as the
**deviation of an observation from its population mean**,
is the difference between an observed value $y$ and its population mean:

$$\devn(y) \eqdef y - \Expf{Y}$$ {#eq-def-model-error}
:::

:::{.notes}
We use the same notation for model error/noise
that we used for [errors](probability.qmd#def-error):
$\devn(y)$.

$\Expf{Y}$ can be viewed as an estimate of $Y$, before $y$ is observed.
Conversely, each observation $y$ can be viewed as an estimate of $\Expf{Y}$
(albeit an imprecise one, individually, since $n=1$).

:::

We can rearrange @eq-def-model-error
to view $y$ as
the sum of its mean plus the model error/noise:

$$y = \Exp{Y} + \epsf{y}$$
